summary: A compact defensive structure featuring four tall wooden corner towers with overhanging dark wood roofs, connected by lower brick and wood walls. The building has a rectangular footprint with a central open courtyard area. Multiple small windows are distributed across the walls, and the structure sits on a stone brick foundation. The design suggests a fortified gatehouse or small castle.

shape.overall_form: rectangular with four corner towers and central courtyard

rebuild_hints: Build four tall wooden towers at each corner, approximately 8-10 blocks tall
Connect towers with lower walls (3-4 blocks high) using brick and wood
Add overhanging dark wood roof sections on all four towers extending 2-3 blocks outward
Create a central open courtyard area between the walls
Place small windows (1-2 blocks) on tower sides and wall sections
Use stone brick for the foundation layer around entire perimeter
Add wooden wall panels as primary wall material with brick accents
Ensure bilateral symmetry across both axes
Include small roof overhangs on tower caps using dark wood slabs
uncertainties: Exact interior layout and courtyard details not fully visible from exterior views
Precise window placement and count uncertain due to viewing angles
Whether structure has a full second floor or partial upper level unclear
Exact brick-to-wood ratio in wall construction not definitively determinable
Roof material composition (slabs vs full blocks) requires closer inspection
Total block count and exact dimensions estimated from visual proportions
